ops.title: Effort level: xhigh default vs max tradeoff
ops.body:
# Effort level: xhigh default vs max tradeoff

Measurement data (AA 2026-07-14) showed `max` effort consumes ~50% of output tokens on thinking for routine subagent tasks; `xhigh` delivers a better cost-quality tradeoff.

**Guidance:**
- Fleet `settings.json` pins `xhigh` as the default effort as of 2026-07-14 (the old `max` pin made thinking roughly half of every implementing subagent's output).
- Reserve `max` for escalation only: after an `xhigh` attempt falls short, not as a starting tier.
- Fable-lane implementation may run `high`.
- Verification runs at the same or higher model + effort tier than the work it checks.
- Same-day measurement: implementing opus subagents put 73–86% of wall-clock into generation, roughly half of it thinking, under the old `max` pin — the motivating observation for the switch.
- gpt-5.6-sol runs `xhigh` only: at `max` its first-token latency inverts to 131s vs opus's 40s, so `max` is not a sol escalation rung (a hard miss escalates to fable instead).
ops.when: when tuning default effort levels or optimizing agent/workflow resource allocation
